Event/Activity: Annunciation: Angel Gabriel announces Jesus’ birth to Mary
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Fulfillment of prophecy; divine conception. Only in Luke.

Event/Activity: Mary visits Elizabeth (John the Baptist’s mother)
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Mary’s Magnificat. Only in Luke.

Event/Activity: Birth of Jesus; Angels, shepherds, Magi visit
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Luke: shepherds, manger; Matthew: Magi, house.

Event/Activity: Presentation at the Temple; Simeon and Anna’s prophecies
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in Luke; fulfills law, prophetic recognition.

Event/Activity: Herod’s massacre of infants; Flight to Egypt
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in Matthew; fulfills prophecy.

Event/Activity: Flight to Egypt to escape Herod’s massacre
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in Matthew; “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
Both Gospels agree on Nazareth as home.

Event/Activity: Jesus at age 12 in the Temple
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in Luke; Jesus’ awareness of divine mission.

Event/Activity: Jesus’ youth and early adulthood as carpenter
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Preparation for ministry.
All Gospels; voice from heaven, Spirit descends.
All Synoptics; order of temptations varies.

Event/Activity: First disciples called (Andrew, Peter, Philip, Nathanael)
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in John; Synoptics describe call by Sea of Galilee.

Event/Activity: First miracle: water to wine at wedding
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in John; first “sign.”
All Gospels; many miracles and teachings.
John: early in ministry; Synoptics: at end.

Event/Activity: Night meeting with Nicodemus; early Judean ministry
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in John; teaching on new birth.

Event/Activity: Woman at the well; Samaritan revival
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Only in John; first major outreach beyond Jews.
All Gospels; “Kingdom of God” theme.

Event/Activity: Call of Peter, Andrew, James, John; miracles on the lake
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Synoptics: call of fishermen; John: feeding 5,000, walking on water.

Event/Activity: Sermon on the Mount/Plain; teaching parables
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Synoptics: ethical teaching, Beatitudes.
Only in Luke; authority over death.
Mark: Gerasene/Gadarene demoniac; regional variation.
Synoptics; variations in location names.
Only in Mark; unique two-stage healing.
All Synoptics; first open acknowledgment of Messiahship.
All Synoptics; divine confirmation of Jesus’ identity.
All Synoptics; Zacchaeus only in Luke.
Only in John; triggers plot to kill Jesus.
Only in John; safety from Pharisees’ plot.
All Synoptics; fulfillment of Zech. 9:9.

Event/Activity: Triumphal Entry, temple cleansing, teaching, Last Supper, betrayal, trial, crucifixion
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: All Gospels; John’s chronology differs (Passover timing).
All Gospels; Judas leads arresting party; Synoptics: “kiss of betrayal.”

Event/Activity: Trials before Jewish and Roman authorities
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: All Gospels; Peter’s denials; Pilate/Herod variations (Herod only in Luke1).

Event/Activity: Judas returns silver, hangs himself (Matthew); falls headlong, dies (Acts)
Significance & Notes (incl. variations,: Matthew: Judas hangs himself, priests buy field; Acts: Judas falls, bursts open; field called Akeldama.
All Gospels; details vary (e.g., words from cross, order of events).
All Gospels; guards at tomb only in Matthew.
All Gospels; appearances differ in number, location, sequence. Ascension: Luke/Acts: Bethany/Mount of Olives; Matthew: Galilee.
